channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: 'Not even one beer' for Man City after FA Cup triumph over Chelsea
item.description: Manchester City's FA Cup celebrations after a 1-0 win over Chelsea will be low key, as the team refocuses on its tight Premier League title race with Arsenal.

media:group.media:description: Matheus Nunes may not have got Guardiola's memo.

item.title: Bushwalker takes photo of rare bug in the wild
item.description: There were no known photographs taken in the wild of the Myrmeleon houstoni until a regional Victorian woman snapped a photo of one while on a bushwalk.

media:group.media:description: Judy Allen has taken the first known image of the Myremeleon Houstoni dragonfly in the wild.

item.title: Caldwell becomes second-fastest Aussie with Diamond League podium
item.description: Abbey Caldwell beats four other Australians to third place in the women's 1,500m final on the first night of Diamond League racing for the year.

media:group.media:description: Abbey Caldwell (second from left) is the second-fastest Australian woman over 1,500m.

item.title: Woman killed in bus crash asked to be organ donor just days earlier
item.description: Uqasha Imran was fatally injured when a bus driver struck her on her motorcycle in 2025. Now, her family are promoting the importance of organ donation while helping other young athletes like her.
item.dc:creator: Niki Burnside

media:group.media:description: Imran Amjad says hearing from one of the people his daughter saved has helped the family.

item.title: Gold Coast e-bike crackdown 'not effective', council report finds
item.description: A Gold Coast council report finds an e-bike crackdown has failed to significantly reduce anti-social behaviour, with many riders ignoring compliance officers.
item.dc:creator: Dominic Cansdale

media:group.media:description: The dangerous and illegal use of e-motorbikes has become more prevalent in recent years.

item.title: The darkness of a Tasmanian town's night sky could be protected
item.description: The northern Tasmanian town of Ross is working to protect its night sky, a years-long process that would see it formally recognised for its darkness and clarity.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie Archer

media:group.media:description: If successful, Ross will be the first town in Tasmanian to be accredited by Dark Sky International.

item.title: Welcome to campdraft school, where a new generation takes the reins
item.description: Amid the red dirt and flies of an outback stockyard, thrillseekers with a passion for horses learn about a quintessential Aussie sport.
item.dc:creator: Lily McCure

media:group.media:description: Campdrafting involves a high level of horsemanship, speed and skill.

item.title: First Nations art collective calls for industry inquiry to protect artists
item.description: APY Art Centre Collective members say a voluntary code of conduct isn't protecting artists from financial exploitation and misconduct in the industry.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Carbone
item.category: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

media:group.media:description: The collective has art centres throughout regional South Australia and a studio gallery in Adelaide.

item.title: From cask to car, could red wine be Australia's next fuel?
item.description: Australia's wine industry is investigating whether its current 263 million litre glut of wine would be better used as biofuel to power cars, trucks or even aircraft.
item.dc:creator: Timu King

media:group.media:description: While wine cannot be used as fuel, distilling it could provide a source of biofuel for cars.

item.title: Taiwan remains committed to status quo after Trump's China trip
item.description: The Taiwanese government responds after US President Donald Trump's trip to China and his call for the two countries to "cool down".
item.dc:creator: Fergus Gregg

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump says he wants both China and Taiwan to "cool down".

item.title: Former ABC journalist on loving life at 100 and competing in athletics
item.description: Former ABC journalist and television executive Allan Martin details his renewed zest for life thanks to competing in athletics — all at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Digby Werthmuller and Sarah Gerathy

media:group.media:description: Allan Martin at the 2023 Australian Masters Games in Adelaide.

item.title: Major highways left in the dark as copper theft surges in Queensland
item.description: The practice is costing Queensland ratepayers millions of dollars in repairs, and leading to widespread outages to lighting, communications and community sporting facilities.
item.dc:creator: Tobi Loftus

media:group.media:description: Parts of this motorway went dark in 2026, believed to be due to copper thieves stripping away wiring from street lights.

item.title: 'Crisis situation': The Allan government is scrambling to remake donation laws
item.description: With only six months before the state election, the Victorian government struggles to devise new donation laws after the High Court found previous laws unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Richard Willingham and Rachel Eddie

media:group.media:description: Jacinta Allan's government is scrambling to draft new donations laws ahead of the upcoming state election.

item.title: ‘His music is still alive’: Pride and sadness in posthumous Gurrumul honour
item.description: Nearly a decade since his death, Gurrumul is set to be inducted into the ARIA Hall of Fame, bringing pride and reflection to a remote part of Australia.
item.dc:creator: Matt Garrick

media:group.media:description: Gurrumul is set to be posthumously inducted into the ARIA Hall of Fame.

item.title: First home buyers 'up and about' at auctions after negative gearing changes
item.description: The government says the changes are aimed at levelling the playing field for young buyers bidding against property investors, while the opposition is pledging to undo the reforms, citing their potential to raise rents.
item.dc:creator: Selina Bryan

media:group.media:description: One auctioneer says younger buyers are "up and about" in response to the government's tax reforms.

item.title: Divers who faced jail for cultural fishing lead sea urchin recovery
item.description: After decades of fighting for their traditional fishing rights, Aboriginal custodians are now fighting to save their sea country from ecological collapse.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Milton and Toby Hemmings

media:group.media:description: Walbunja divers John and Denzel Carriage were facing jail time for cultural fishing.

item.title: Sam Kerr bids emotional Chelsea farewell with record-equalling goal
item.description: Sam Kerr ties the record for the most Women's Super League goals for Chelsea as she plays her final match with the London club after more than six years.

media:group.media:description: Sam Kerr and injured English star Millie Bright waved goodbye to Chelsea.

item.title: China quietly turns off supply of world's most used industrial chemical
item.description: Amid disruption from the Iran war, China has banned exports of sulphuric acid, a crucial component used to treat drinking water, refine petroleum and make batteries, clothing and phosphate fertilisers.
item.dc:creator: Megan Hughes

media:group.media:description: LoomTex relies on multiple types of acid in its production process.

item.title: Push to make rental properties ready for portable power future
item.description: A standard power plug for charging your home from an EV is among the measures proposed to improve energy standards for rental properties.
item.dc:creator: Justin Huntsdale

media:group.media:description: Robin Sands has installed portable solar at his unit and charges his electric vehicle at home.

item.title: Disabled Australians on love, lust and the right to intimacy
item.description: It's a stormy February night in Adelaide, and Jacqueline Tedmanson is at the hospital with her partner Jackson, waiting to meet their first baby.

media:group.media:description: Jacqueline Tedmanson, aka Diana Divine, five months pregnant, still burlesque dancing and showing how mobility aids can be really sexy.

item.title: 'You're malnourished; you are overtrained': Olympic gymnast's warning
item.description: Alexandra Kiroi-Bogatyreva trained up to 10 hours a day, six days a week, often without the proper nutrition. She wants others to know what that can do to the body.
item.dc:creator: Johanna McDiarmid

media:group.media:description: Alexandra Kiroi-Bogatyreva got her first period at 19, and says no one questioned why.

item.title: Why some Australian homebuyers are turning to Islamic financing
item.description: When Wildan decided to buy a property he didn't even consider a conventional loan. Instead, he opted for a niche option that caters to a growing segment of Australia's population.
item.dc:creator: Raihanah Rasum

media:group.media:description: Wildan Al Hazmi says he chose Islamic financing for "peace of mind".

item.title: Aspiration has changed since the Howard era. This budget is finally catching up
item.description: At the turn of the century, "aspiration" described Australians who wanted to put their kids into private school and university, and to build their wealth by getting an investment property or two. Today, it means something humbler; millions of younger Australians simply aspire to own a home one day, to have some shelter.
item.dc:creator: Gareth Hutchens

media:group.media:description: Prime Minister John Howard and Treasurer Peter Costello hold a press conference on November 18, 2007, a week before they lost the 2007 election.

item.title: Trump is painting DC's reflecting pool. Here's why people are angry
item.description: Donald Trump has chosen a new colour for Washington DC's Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. Here are three reasons it's proving to be controversial.
item.dc:creator: Brad Ryan

media:group.media:description: The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool will soon be "American flag blue", Donald Trump says.

item.title: As a child Rob was burnt. As an adult, he’s fighting fire with fire
item.description: Every time farmer and volunteer firefighter Rob Armstrong gets a call to battle a bushfire, something inside says not to do it. But he goes anyway. He knows what it’s like to be trapped in the flames and rescued by volunteers.

media:group.media:description: Victorian farmer Rob Armstrong has been a volunteer firefighter for 40 years.
